$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `conversation_annotations` repo
`prompt_templates`). One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness conversation_annotations tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: a REAL-affinity unbounded-int column (`messageIndex` —
`z.number().int().min(0)` with NO `.max()`, so v4's mapToSQLiteType assigns
REAL, not INTEGER; bound as f64, the integer-valued cell collapses back to an
integer in the canonical dump) and a nullable UUID column (`sourceMessageId`,
exercised both null and non-null). Scope: create / update / delete only.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the folders,
tags, text-replacement-rules, and prompt-templates fixtures use. It is NOT the
real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

seed.content: An opening flourish, unattributed to any source message.
A later remark, tied to a specific source message.

ops.comment: New annotation with NO source message (sourceMessageId null). messageIndex 0 exercises the REAL-affinity integer-collapse. id + timestamps pinned.

data.content: A fresh aside on a different chat.

ops.comment: New annotation WITH a source message (sourceMessageId set) and a larger messageIndex.

data.content: An annotation anchored to message seven.

ops.comment: Update seed annotation #1: revise the content. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.

data.content: An opening flourish, now revised on a later pass.

ops.comment: Update seed annotation #2: re-anchor it to a different messageIndex (still an integer -> REAL-affinity collapse).
